remember: Students will be able to recall the importance of reproductive health in humans.
Students will be able to describe the causes of various reproductive health disorders.
Students will be able to list the measures for the prevention of reproductive health disorders.
understand: Students will be able to explain the physiological changes during puberty in males and females.
Students will be able to describe the menstrual cycle and the factors affecting it.
Students will be able to understand the various reproductive health problems faced by women.
apply: Students will be able to apply knowledge of reproductive health to prevent and manage reproductive health disorders.
Students will be able to use knowledge of menstrual cycles to plan and manage menstrual health.
Students will be able to design a plan for the prevention of reproductive health disorders.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the causes of reproductive health disorders and suggest possible solutions.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different methods of contraception.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of various reproductive health programs.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT, students will be able to understand the concept of reproductive health, its importance and the factors affecting it.
Students will be able to describe the reproductive health problems in males and females and their consequences.
board_exam_objectives: Students will be able to explain the importance of reproductive health and the measures for its promotion.
Students will be able to describe the physiological changes during puberty in males and females and their significance.
Students will be able to explain the causes and prevention of reproductive health disorders.
